Journal.Title: Lin chuang er bi yan hou tou jing wai ke za zhi = Journal of clinical otorhinolaryngology head and neck surgery
Journal.ISOAbbreviation: Lin Chuang Er Bi Yan Hou Tou Jing Wai Ke Za Zhi

Article.ArticleTitle: [The influence of microvessel density and microlymphatic vessel density on prognosis in hypopharyngeal carcinoma and the construction].

Abstract.AbstractText: Objective:This study aims to investigate the influence of microvessel density（MVD） and microlymphatic vessel density（MLVD） on the prognosis of patients with hypopharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma（HPSCC） and to develop a nomogram prediction model for prognosis based on pathological characteristics. Methods:A retrospective analysis was conducted on clinicopathological and follow-up data from HPSCC patients who underwent surgical treatment at our institution between June 2010 and June 2020. Immunohistochemical staining was performed on tumor tissues and adjacent normal margin tissues to evaluate MVD and MLVD. The associations among MVD, MLVD, and clinicopathological features were analyzed. Univariate and multivariate Cox regression analyses were conducted to identify independent risk factors affecting overall survival（OS）. Based on these findings, a nomogram model was constructed and its predictive accuracy was assessed using C-index, receiver operating characteristic（ROC） curve, and calibration curve. Results:Both MVD and MLVD were significantly higher in HPSCC tumor tissues compared to normal tissues. Patients in the high MVD and high MLVD groups exhibited significantly lower OS rates than those in the low MVD and low MLVD groups. Multivariate Cox regression analysis revealed that N stage, recurrence, nerve invasion, lymph node capsule invasion, MVD, and MLVD were independent prognostic factors of OS. Based on these factors, a nomogram prognosis model was successfully constructed. The nomograms demonstrated superior performance in terms of C-index, area under the ROC curve, and calibration, outperforming the AJCC TNM staging system. Conclusion:Elevated MVD and MLVD levels are associated with poorer prognosis in HPSCC patients. The nomogram model based on pathological features provides valuable insights for clinical assessment and decision-making.
Abstract.CopyrightInformation: Copyright© by the Editorial Department of Journal of Clinical Otorhinolaryngology Head and Neck Surgery.

MedlineJournalInfo.MedlineTA: Lin Chuang Er Bi Yan Hou Tou Jing Wai Ke Za Zhi

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: 目的：探讨微血管密度（microvessel density，MVD）、微淋巴管密度（microlymphatic vessel density，MLVD）对下咽鳞状细胞癌（hypoparyngeal squamous cell carcinoma，HPSCC）患者预后的影响，并基于病理特征构建预后预测模型。 方法：回顾性分析2010年6月至2020年6月就诊于我院并行手术治疗的HPSCC患者的临床病理资料及随访资料，对HPSCC肿瘤组织及切缘正常组织蜡块行免疫组织化学染色，分析MVD和MLVD与HPSCC临床病理特征的关系，通过单因素及多因素Cox回归分析影响HPSCC患者术后总生存期（overall survival，OS）的危险因素，将独立预后影响因素纳入并构建预后模型，采用C指数（C-index）、受试者工作特征（receiver operating characteristic，ROC）曲线和校准曲线对模型的预测预后准确性进行评价与验证。 结果：MVD、MLVD在HPSCC肿瘤组织中呈高表达，且高MVD组、高MLVD组患者OS显著低于低MVD组、低MLVD组。单因素和多因素Cox回归分析显示，N分期、肿瘤复发、神经侵犯、淋巴结包膜侵犯、MVD及MLVD均为影响OS的独立危险因素。基于以上结果构建HPSCC的1、3和5年OS的列线图模型，该模型在C指数、ROC曲线下面积和校准曲线方面表现良好，并且在预测OS方面优于AJCC TNM分期系统。 结论：MVD、MLVD显著影响HPSCC患者预后，表达越高提示患者预后越差。基于病理特征构建的HPSCC预后列线图模型有利于临床评估和指导临床决策。.
OtherAbstract.CopyrightInformation: Copyright© by the Editorial Department of Journal of Clinical Otorhinolaryngology Head and Neck Surgery.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors of this article and the planning committee members and staff have no relevant financial relationships with commercial interests to disclose.
